Facet: Gossamer, Inertia, Key, Miasma, Trial, Yonder
Narrative_Meaning: Aces signify beginnings, meetings and unions. They can indicate love-interests, chance encounters, alliances, and friendships.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Threshold Card represents doorways, portals and liminal spaces. It is the edge of something else, a place where one goes from one place to another, often with different rules. It is a card of paradigm shifts, of new places. Great changes, events and dangers may lie ahead, and it is not too late to turn back, but then fresh understandings will not be gained, and no new discoveries will be made.

Significator.Plot: The Threshold Plot is often one of invasion. Of course, who the invaders are, and how they invade varies from Plot to Plot. Threshold Plots can be about stepping into another world (Portal Fantasies), exploring strange new lands, uninvited guests, Færy visitors, hauntings, or space invaders.
Significator.Side: A side marked by the Threshold is marked as liminal somehow, they may be on the cusp of a decision perhaps whether to ally with one or the other of the other sides, or they may have contacts beyond this Story, or even world, then again they may be a group of Heroes or Yarn-Tellers that have Alternates in another Era or universe.
Significator.Character:
The Threshold Significator indicates a Character who is between two worlds, perhaps on the cusp between one thing and another.

They Gain Yin when they act confused, tired, or shy.

They Gain Yang when they act like a know-it-all, or act arrogant or confident.

They Gain Chi when they cross a Threshold, enter a new space, or notice the time (usually by dusk or dawn).

They gain +1 Success Levels to all actions in liminal spaces and times (at dusk, midnight, new years, doorways, crossroads etc)
Significator.Scene: The Threshold Scene usually represents some portal or barrier that must be overcome or penetrated. A Yarn-Teller may play the card to create a portal or other connection between Locations, which may be permanent or temporary as required, or may play the card to create a Threshold that acts as a barrier that must be overcome with a Test, Ordeal or Quest. A truly crafty Yarn-Teller could even send someone to another lifetime as a distraction.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Hitches, the bigger the better.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Story opens with action, often a Fight, it could be the PCs who kick in a door, or an enemy may burst in.
Yarn.Fray_Description: This is as bad as a Threshold can get, it is the liminal space between the known and the unknown. It might be the edge of a map, the ocean over the horizon, the edge of space, or the mouth of a cave. Usually such portals and thresholds will have some sort of guardian that must be defeated, sneaked or riddled past with an Ordeal.
Yarn.Snag_Description: Sometimes the Snag is due to the time which adds some extra complications to the situation. Perhaps the faltering dusk light makes it harder to be spotted, or the first light of dawn may be what shows your silhouettes to the enemy. Then again the night of first frosts, first snows, or the first day of the thaw are all good complications too).

NPC.Description: The Hero is always the first to volunteer, and the last to quit, they are the star of many an 80's action movie.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: A quiet moment, almost an aside from the plot, that allows the PCs a breather before they push on. It usually occurs before some major event, while opening a door, or entering or exiting a space, and is often marked by one of the Plot's Motifs. They may have fallen in a dark hole, but upon climbing out, the PCs see a butterfly.
Yarn.History_Description: The Threshold indicates that the history of the current situation is a literal beginning, this may mean that the current situation is just begun (it has no true past), or that it can be traced back to a single instant that coincides with the the beginning of something else, such as the birth of a child, dawning of new age, or crowning of a monarch, etc.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Threshold indicates that the current situation is balanced upon the brink of something new, that is only partially known (such as you may know when you are on the brink of a war, but not what the actual war would become). This is a delicate time, where the slightest push may send the situation spiralling out of control.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Threshold indicates that the solution to the current situation involves a crossing, this may mean that you must cross to the other side, and join the opposition, or one of them must cross to you, or that you must leave this place behind to solve the issue.

Revelations.About_Description: The information revealed is about the Dominant Conflict Embodiment. Sometimes they are Characters, but almost as often they are Locations, Quests, Ordeals, or similar. Where this is true the Revelation should reveal details about future Quests the Pressed Characters must complete, or places they should visit. The Revelation can be little more than a call-back or foreshadowing if handled correctly.
Revelations.Info_Description: The information is about the connections that the character or location has.
Revelations.Vector_Description: Auras are for some people a spiritual or emotional energy field that they see, or they may just be another word for a fragrance.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information may be about the Sacrifices the Embodiment has made, or Sacrifices that will have to be made. These Sacrifices might be great and grand, or just toll for the tunnel, or it could just be information about a Sacrifice Umbral.
Revelations.Detail_Description: A target detail is simply that, where, what, or whom the target of the Embodiment is.

Obstacle.Description: The way is blocked by what may be just a closed, locked, barred, or trapped door, airlock, gateway or it could be a portal to another realm. Portals have a tendency to be a bit final, especially when you open them when you don't know where they go.

Tarot.Normal: Automatically adds +21 Pips to any Attack made by the Target. Stakes are Extreme while atop Pile. All Hitches created by or on the Target are "Dying", "Dead" or "Destroyed"
Tarot.Crossed: Automatically adds +21 Pips to any Defence. Stakes are Low while atop Pile. All Hitches created by or on the Target cannot be "Dying", "Dead" or "Destroyed".

Ordeal.Stage_Description: Maybe the buildings are collapsing, or an earthquake is happening. You know the sort of thing, jets of flame leap from the ground, the floor bucks and shakes, power cables spark and fall. Danger is all around.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: The smoke and flames are thick, but you can still see the headlights behind you. They aren't giving up...
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Ebbing enemy have gone for a pincer tactic and have the Flowing forces pinned between groups that are closing in. The Flowing side has a choice, Good mobility and speed will let you escape the trap, or you can hunker down and hope you can smash them as they come. Either way offers the Flowing side +1 Pips per card
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
This is a special Extreme Stakes Ambush, where the Ambushers actually rapidly assault (rather than being hidden). Shock Attackers have +1 Success Level to all rolls and cards during the opening Phase. Although they are rarely more than a Cast Extra, they may be Vex Monsters. With 1 Monster Facet
  Facet    Monster  
Gossamer ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Wiry — The Monster is immensely physically powerful, although often its form may not reveal it. It often demonstrates supernatural power as well as strength and toughness. A small, strong Goblin, a robot that looks like a little girl, a cybernetically enhanced rat, or even a killer doll (when not an Object Monster) are all examples of a Gossamer Monster. The Wiry Monster can add Gossamer Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever it is underestimated, cared for, or demonstrates its strength. A Wiry monster may add their current Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any action with their strength or toughness. Wiries must spend cards from their Monster Pool as though it was a Prepared Defence to ignore damage.
Inertia ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Unstoppable — The Monster is inexorable, it does not stop and cannot be killed by any normal means. They start play with a Monster Pool of Inertia Score cards. Unstoppable Monsters can add Inertia Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cross a threshold, pass a barrier, or are forced to stop or turn around. Unstoppable Monsters can ignore a number of Wounds equal to the number of cards in their Monster Pool. Wounds stacked beyond the Monster Pool affect them as normal. Inexorable Monsters can also ignore all Hitches with a Boon less than their Monster Pool Size. Whenever an Unstoppable Monster is damaged beyond this limit, it must Discard the Highest Card in the Monster Pool, it may immediately also Discard any Wounds lower than discarded Monster Card, any remaining Wounds are then discarded on a one Monster Pool card per Wound basis. However, while discarding these wounds and cards the Monster must remain still (playing dead) until a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Number of Monster Pool cards Discarded (the initial card is instant, the next discard takes an action, then a second takes a few seconds, the third Wound takes an Ordeal Round, 25 Wounds would take a week). Examples of the Inertial Monster type include the Hungry Dead (Zombies) and most robotic assassins (especially ones from the future).
Key ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Hunter — The Monster is a hunter or tracker, it should favour harrying attacks and hit and run tactics. Typically such a Monster will vanish or flee if it is wounded, only to reappear when healed. The hunter may be fooled by false trails, but never for long. Hunters may add Key Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they spot a track, clue, or similar, that indicates the subject of their hunt. Hunters receive a bonus to their Score or Pips equal to the number of cards in the Monster Pool on any tracking, attacking or hunting actions if they also spend a card from the Monster Pool on the action. Hunters may convert Mortal and Carnage Wounds that they take into Crippling Wounds instead, at a cost of one card from their Monster Pool. In T13, the archetypal version of this Monster is a Bulmäs Pack member from the Wild Hunt, but any Werewolf or Animal Shifter would probably qualify, as would robot cowboys in theme parks that have gone wrong, and any fantasy world’s Elven ranger.
Miasma ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Mutant — The monster is a mutant or chimerical creature made from parts of others. This monster type is the oldest perhaps, you’ll find examples throughout the ancient world, particularly in Greek myth. The Mutant will often be larger, and may be malformed or unsymmetrical, with odd numbers of limbs, eyes, horns, or even heads. The Chimera will blend multiple animals together to create a new creature that combines features of all the “parent” creatures, preserving the best of all species. Mutant Monsters start with Miasma Score Monster Pool. They may add Miasma Draw cards to the Pool every time they accomplish a specified goal, such as eating a human, consuming a soul, bathing in radiation, or just growing a year older, etc. The Monster Pool can be used as a “Mutation” Annex, which can be used to mimic the abilities of the Mutants “parent” creatures, such as a spider’s ability to climb, or an eagle’s ability to fly. The Mutation Annex has a Boon equal to the Value of the Number of Cards in the Monster Pool, and so draws cards equal to the Monster Pool Size Reduced and plays a number of cards equal to the Miasma Draw. The Mutation Annex is often used as a Size Annex for the Mutant. Where a 5 card Monster Pool would create a Boon 16 Annex, and could grant a Kaiju Mutant the size of a Skyscraper. 1950s B-movie monsters are often of this type, giant ants, lizards, spiders, wasps, and even rabbits have been used. Classically of course, Cyclopses, Centaurs, Minotaurs, Satyrs, Gryphons (or Griffins), Hippogriffs, Dragons, Sphinxes, Manticores and of course the Chimera itself are all examples. You can also find examples in superheroes, especially those created by radioactive arachnids, or those with an X-gene.
Trial ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Killer — The Killer Monster is a deadly Combat specialist of some sort. Killers add Trial Draw cards to their Monster Pool each time they kill. Killers automatically add +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to all attacks, and Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) is automatically added to the Pips of any attack as Penetration. Killers must spend Monster Pool cards whenever they will make a Trick either with other cards being played by the Ordeal Pool, or other cards in the Round Pool or a Style Reserve. Killers can be drug-addled assassins, crazed shooters, or just steel-souled soldiers.
Yonder ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Alien — The Monster is an alien, an outsider. It may just be a metaphor for alienation, or literally be an extra-(or ultra)-terrestrial. Such monsters are usually obvious and inhuman, not just to look at, but in the way they think and behave. They may have apparently supernatural powers and abilities, but often make do with advanced technologies (in the form of additional Descendants). They may add Yonder Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they are observed doing their alien / inhuman / weird actions. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any action to do with their alien abilities, technologies, motion or survival (including Dodge, Soak, etc). Alien Monsters must play cards from their Monster Pool to socialise with “non-aliens”. From little grey Eusaiveans, through pod people and pepper-pot shaped engines of hate, all the way to face-hugging space-wasps, there are plenty of aliens.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Notes: The Paths of the All-Walkers can allow Bulmäs to do extraordinary things, like walking an individual’s life-path, so that all of their personal history is known to them. All-Walkers can assume even the forms and souls of other people by using these paths, without directly eating of the Soul. All-Walkers can even Doom Weave and play other cards from their hands as Wyrd Tarot cards, once they have played an Ace on themselves.

Stress.Description: Sometimes a Character is neither one thing or the other, in neither one place or the other, and this can make it difficult for them to act
Stress.Rules: Character must discard all Spades (♠) cards from Ordeal Pool, Style Reserves and Wyrd Tarot Hand, until they have zero Stress. Each Spade Discarded removes 1 Stress.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can push a Character to their breaking point, to the very edge of sanity, or to borderline of the beyond. Some Characters never quite come back after they have been that far, they forever seem to exist in the liminal space at the edge of rational thought, neither fully rational or irrational, and never certain of anything again. To some they may seem to have retreated into a fantasy world, but some others will note that events from the real world are seemlessly integrated into their fantasies, and often this fantasy may offer deeper insights into what is going on than the "real-world" can suppy.
Trauma.Rules: Trauma pushes a character to the edge of their sanity. Creating a Psychosocial Location (purchased with Relieved Stress acting as Chi) within their mind that may have overlaps or connections to any other point of the Psychosocial mindscape (which will affect the nature of the Location — for example placing it deep in the Dreamscape may make it seem fantastical, connecting it to Supernatural may allow it limited telepathy, even allowing the Character to retreat beyond their own mindscape for safety in another’s mind. The Character can thereafter Relieve 1 Stress instantly by retreating to this Psychosocial Location, or by acting as though they are Uncertain (what is real, what is right, what they shoud do, etc).

Age.Description: An Age of Emergence is occurring. During this age society stands upon the brink of a great sea-change in thinking, some paradigm shift is underway, and after the age has finished some aspect of society will be forever altered. The whole society will be affected and altered by this shift in perspective, historically these revolutionary ideas have caused huge changes in culture and the path of history, the industrial revolution changed how the world worked, the assembly line altered businesses and factories forever, the internet altered communications and information retrieval completely.
Age.Rules: During an Age of Emergence some new paradigm, theory or technology sweeps the world, changing all societies. Societies that fail to adapt to the Emerging new world usually swiftly collapse, hoever those that can exploit some new niche of the new world quickly thrive.
